description: Aechmea distichantha, the Brazilian vaseplant, or vase plant, is a bromeliad typical of Cerrado vegetation in Brazil, which is also native to northern Argentina, Bolivia, Paraguay, and Uruguay. This plant is often used as an ornamental plant.

description: The urn plant (Aechmea fasciata) is an amazing species because of its striking foliage and interesting growth habit. It is one of the few bromeliads that produces structural leaves in a rosette pattern and colorful flower spikes. Its evergreen leaves can range from deep green to silver and can feature stripes, spots, or variegation. Additionally, this species is known to produce offsets, which makes it easier to propagate. And, finally, its vivid red, pink, or yellow flowers, depending on the variety, are stunning and last for months.
synonyms: ["Billbergia glazioviana", "Platyaechmea fasciata var. flavivittata", "Aechmea rhodocyanea", "Hoplophytum fasciatum", "Platyaechmea fasciata", "Platyaechmea fasciata var. purpurea", "Platyaechmea fasciata var. pruinosa", "Aechmea fasciata var. pruinosa", "Aechmea hamata", "Billbergia fasciata"]

care.fertilizer_type: Dilute fertilizers following instructions,  apply liquid fertilizers once every 10-15 days in summer and autumn

It is one of the few bromeliads that produces structural leaves in a rosette patter | Similar plants: Hoya curtisii | Native to: BZL, CARIBBEAN, BZL ES, BZL TR, SOUTHERN AMERICA
common_problems: Monitor for scale and mealybugs. Overwatering can cause root rot. Leaf spot and crown rot can also occur. Brown leaf tips can occur if the plant gets inadequate water or is exposed to cold temperatures. Insects can breed in the trapped water in the central cup if not emptied and rinsed regularly.

description: The pineapple is a tropical plant with an edible fruit; it is the most economically significant plant in the family Bromeliaceae.
origin: Costa Rica to South Tropical America

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun
care.soil_types: Peat or soil with specific nutrients
care.fertilizer_type: Dilute fertilizers following instructions,  apply liquid fertilizers once every 10-15 days in summer and autumn

care.tips: Remove old, diseased, dead leaves and withered flowers timely | Similar plants: Cryptanthus bivittatus | Native to: BZN, PER, MLY, GUY, CAY
common_problems: Wilt and root rot can be significant

Fruit production or ornamental plant for tropical and other frost free areas
Warm greenhouses or houseplant for temperate regions

common_pests: Watch for nematodes, scale, thrips and mealybugs
care.used_for: ["Pineapple bran and by-products of pineapple processing have been used in livestock feed.  Bromelain extracted from pineapple may be used as a meat tenderizer.  The stems and leaves are a source of fiber and can be processed into paper or 'pina cloth'."]
care.used_for_details: Pineapple bran and by-products of pineapple processing have been used in livestock feed.  Bromelain extracted from pineapple may be used as a meat tenderizer.  The stems and leaves are a source of fiber and can be processed into paper or 'pina cloth'.
care.edible_parts: Sweet, succulent, aromatic mature fruit edible raw, cooked or preserved.

description: Cryptanthus (group) is an amazing plant species that is known for its wide variety of leaf shapes, textures and colors. Each plant forms a perfect rosette that can range from light green, to bright, and even hues of pink. The foliage is always wavy and scalloped, truly making these plants one of a kind and amazing. Furthermore, the petite size of these plants makes them ideal for tabletop decorations or sprucing up any space with bright, unique foliage. Whether the Cryptanthus (group) is placed in an indoor or outdoor setting, it is sure to draw attention and admiration from its viewers.

description: Tillandsia recurvata, commonly known as small ballmoss or ball moss, is a flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae that grows upon larger host plants. It grows well in areas with low light, little airflow, and high humidity, which is commonly provided by southern shade trees, often the southern live oak. It is not a parasite like mistletoe, but an epiphyte like its relative Spanish moss.

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun

description: Tillandsia schiedeana is a species of flowering plant in the genus, Tillandsia. It was named for the collector Christian Julius Wilhelm Schiede. As an epiphyte, it is found "growing in open tropical forests, and saxicolous, growing on cacti and burseras on steep dry slopes in semiarid regions in Mexico, Central America, West Indies, Venezuela, and Colombia at elevations of 750 to 5,500 feet."
